source_jobs.title: Senior Client Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Cylinder

Founded in 2021 and offered as an employer benefit, Cylinder (formerly Vivante Health) delivers personalized, clinician-backed care to those suffering from digestive issues through its virtual health platform. Cylinder’s tech-forward, human-first program connects members to the right level of support at the right time through an easy interface, bringing the healthcare ecosystem together to align around member needs. Cylinder’s dedicated care teams help members proactively address their gut health from every angle – decreasing costs, reducing absenteeism, and improving their quality of life, at scale. Cylinder’s vision is to create a world where people receive convenient, personalized, and affordable access to the quality digestive healthcare they need to improve their overall health.

Position Summary

As a Senior Client Success Manager at Cylinder Health, you will manage a portfolio of some of our high value, high growth enterprise level clients. You will lead all key activities including ongoing service delivery, relationship building at the executive and lower levels, account strategy development, project expansion to meet growth targets and contract renewals. You will coordinate closely with your clients and marketing to develop communication strategies that drive member enrollment. You will serve as the connective tissue between Cylinder Clients and key internal stakeholders such as Product and Engineering, Finance, Sales, Member Services and Clinical Operations.  This position demands a consultative approach, strong project management skills, and a commitment to delivering exceptional client experiences. This is an individual contributor role with the potential to grow.

Responsibilities Include:

 - Account Management & Client Success
   
   - Provide exceptional account management throughout the project and contract lifecycle, ensuring continuous client satisfaction and support.
   
   - Cultivate strong relationships across all levels within client organizations, including main points of contact and decision-makers, to foster partnership success.
   
   - Drive annual revenue retention and growth by developing and executing account plans aligned with client and company objectives.

 - Data-Driven Strategy & ROI Communication
   
   - Leverage data insights to inform and adapt account strategies.
   
   - Interpret and effectively communicate ROI and clinical metrics to clients, highlighting their crucial value to both their organization and the Cylinder partnership.

 - Marketing & Engagement
   
   - Lead the delivery of marketing campaigns that boost enrollment and engagement, ultimately contributing to client satisfaction.
   
   - Collaborate with the Cylinder marketing team, utilizing your expertise to tailor existing materials and co-create new campaigns that address specific client needs.

You'll be a good fit here if you are:

 - Self-motivated and passionate about the employer health benefits space with a desire to push yourself and continue learning

 - An excellent communicator, a strategic thinker and able to align multiple stakeholders to drive decisions and action

 - Partnerships and sales oriented to hit aggressive goals while still furthering client needs

 - A highly detail oriented individual with an eye for process creation and improvement 

 - Comfortable in a fast-paced, dynamic start up environment

 - Creative, innovative, and willing to share creative suggestions in a team environment

 - Empathetic to our members’ GI health conditions and are driven to improve their outcomes

Desired Qualifications

 - 6+ years of hands-on experience working with self-insured employers, payers, and TPA’s or previous experience in onboarding and ongoing management of large enterprise or strategic clients in a health-oriented industry

 - 6+ years client relationship/account management experience with national payers and/or Fortune 500 employers

 - Proven ability to hit sales goals/quotas

 - S

source_jobs.title: Enrollment Marketing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About Cylinder

Founded in 2021 and offered as an employer benefit, Cylinder (formerly Vivante Health) delivers personalized, clinician-backed care to those suffering from digestive issues through its virtual health platform. Cylinder’s tech-forward, human-first program connects members to the right level of support at the right time through an easy interface, bringing the healthcare ecosystem together to align around member needs. Cylinder’s dedicated care teams help members proactively address their gut health from every angle – decreasing costs, reducing absenteeism, and improving their quality of life, at scale. Cylinder’s vision is to create a world where people receive convenient, personalized, and affordable access to the quality digestive healthcare they need to improve their overall health.

Position Summary

We are looking for an Enrollment Marketing Specialist to join the Member Marketing team. Reporting to the Director of Member Marketing, this role will support the strategy, execution, and optimization of enrollment campaigns that drive member engagement and program adoption. This role partners cross-functionally to deliver data-driven, scalable marketing communications that support client goals, improve conversion outcomes, and contribute to business growth. The ideal candidate is a highly organized, analytical marketer with experience managing complex campaigns in a fast-paced, startup environment.

Responsibilities Include:

 - Support the member enrollment (recruitment) marketing strategy and execution for Cylinder’s clients, including client-specific communications planning, campaign management, and performance tracking

 - Manage the recruitment communications calendar and report out on key campaign metrics, leveraging data, A/B testing, and performance insights to optimize engagement, scalability, and conversion outcomes

 - Partner cross-functionally with Client Success, Marketing Ops, and Data teams to deliver impactful campaigns that drive awareness, support client goals, and contribute to annual revenue targets

 - Identify opportunities to improve and scale processes to increase operational efficiency and campaign effectiveness

 - Lead client-facing communication initiatives by pitching strategies, presenting campaign plans and assets, and maintaining strong relationships with new and existing clients

You'll be a good fit here if you are:

 - Passionate about improving gut health

 - Exceptional attention to detail

 - Self-motivated problem solver

 - Someone who thrives in a fast-paced, remote-first environment

 - Comfortable with fluid job responsibilities

Required Qualifications

 - 2-4 years of work experience with 1-2 years of experience planning and executing marketing campaigns on behalf of clients

 - Bachelor's Degree in Business, Marketing, Healthcare or other related fields

 - Experience working in a B2B2C capacity

 - Comfortable presenting in front of groups of all sizes

 - Self-directed, highly organized, and ability to prioritize

Cylinder is an equal opportunity employer. We believe safe spaces where everyone can be their authentic selves is the key to a successful team so we welcome and embrace all identities, cultures, and backgrounds.

source_jobs.title: Data Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Cylinder

Founded in 2021 and offered as an employer benefit, Cylinder (formerly Vivante Health) delivers personalized, clinician-backed care to those suffering from digestive issues through its virtual health platform. Cylinder’s tech-forward, human-first program connects members to the right level of support at the right time through an easy interface, bringing the healthcare ecosystem together to align around member needs. Cylinder’s dedicated care teams help members proactively address their gut health from every angle – decreasing costs, reducing absenteeism, and improving their quality of life, at scale. Cylinder’s vision is to create a world where people receive convenient, personalized, and affordable access to the quality digestive healthcare they need to improve their overall health.

Position Summary

We are seeking a Data Engineering Manager to play a crucial role in leading the strategy and day-to-day execution for our data engineering organization. Your team will "own" our Data Warehouse, collaborating closely with our Data Science and Software Engineering teams to enhance our product, and support the operational data needs of our strategic Insights, Marketing, and Finance teams. Additionally, you will build and mentor a high-performing team, ensuring that our data systems and solutions align with our mission to improve healthcare outcomes.

This is a hands-on role; the ideal candidate will have high overlap with our Data Engineering toolstack (GCP, DBT, Airflow, Python) and be comfortable supporting their team via regular code reviews and contributing to the code base. This role is also highly collaborative and necessitates working closely with other Engineering teams, and fostering close partnerships with the Product, Clinical, Operations and Finance teams. Your work will empower Cylinder Health to harness the power of data for actionable insights, operational efficiency, and cutting-edge innovations in healthcare.

Why is this a great opportunity for a Data Engineering Manager?

 - Impactful Problem Domain: You’ll drive innovation by integrating and analyzing diverse datasets - including patient health records, lab results, medical claims, and population health data—to create groundbreaking insights that improve healthcare outcomes.

 - Early-Stage Influence: It’s still early enough that there are many decisions and discoveries to be made. This is your opportunity to shape the future of our data ecosystem.

 - Mission-Driven Culture: Join a team that’s passionate about improving clinical outcomes for members and advancing the field of GI health.

Responsibilities Include:

 - Data Engineering Leadership, Strategy & Execution
   
   - Help define and execute on a strategic vision for data engineering that aligns with business goals
   
   - Collaborate with Product and other stakeholders to define and prioritize the technical roadmap, ensuring alignment with business objectives
   
   - Drive the successful execution of data engineering projects across multiple teams, including planning, resource allocation, and project management
   
   - Continue to mature data engineering best practices towards test-based development and automation

 - Data Infrastructure & Engineering
   
   - Oversee the development and maintenance of scalable, reliable data pipelines and infrastructure
   
   - Ensure efficient data ingestion, transformation, and storage solutions that support analytics and machine learning models
   
   - Lead efforts to integrate various data systems and platforms to create a unified patient centered dataset

 - Data Governance
   
   - Establish and enforce best practices for data quality, privacy, security, compliance (e.g., HIPAA) and ethical data usage
   
   - Improve operational efficiency by ensuring a 'single source of truth' for all analytics data

Team Building & Mentorship

 - Build and grow a high-performing team of data engineers

 - Foster a culture of innovation, continuous learning

source_jobs.title: Lead Software Engineer, Backend
source_jobs.description:
About Cylinder

Founded in 2021 and offered as an employer benefit, Cylinder (formerly Vivante Health) delivers personalized, clinician-backed care to those suffering from digestive issues through its virtual health platform. Cylinder's tech-forward, human-first program connects members to the right level of support at the right time through an easy interface, bringing the healthcare ecosystem together to align around member needs. Cylinder's dedicated care teams help members proactively address their gut health from every angle – decreasing costs, reducing absenteeism, and improving their quality of life, at scale. Cylinder's vision is to create a world where people receive convenient, personalized, and affordable access to the quality digestive healthcare they need to improve their overall health.

Position Summary

As a Lead Software Engineer at Cylinder, you will be a technical leader and owner of feature delivery, helping build and scale our digestive health platform. You'll be a hands-on technical leader — comfortable jumping into architecture discussions, code reviews, and technical decisions. A meaningful part of this role involves leading your team in building AI-powered features, collaborating with our data and clinical teams on LLM-based experiences and model integrations. You'll partner closely with product, clinical, and data teams to deliver high-quality software that directly improves outcomes for our members. Our backend services are developed mostly in Go and run in GKE (k8s) or directly on other GCP serverless frameworks, with GitHub and Terraform powering the DevOps environment.

Why is this a great opportunity?

 - The problem domain and our approach to solving it is super compelling. We're bringing together data sources that have never been joined, building models of a GI patient that have never been built, and discovering new GI health insights that help real people to manage and support their real medical conditions.

 - It's still early enough that there's a lot of decisions and new discoveries to be made, so this is your chance to get in early and help shape our future.

Other things about our environment you should be aware of:

 - We're still a small company, which means that everyone still wears a lot of hats and we need engineers who are comfortable with ambiguity and working across a wide variety of challenges. That said, our existing engineering team has built an extremely efficient working environment based on best-in-class hosted SaaS frameworks that greatly minimize the overhead in deploying and supporting software in production.

 - We're all really passionate about improving clinical outcomes for our members and truly advancing the GI health space. Most of us also have personal (direct/family/friend) connections to GI health problems. Whatever it is that fuels you, we're on a mission to build something much larger than ourselves here and looking for another partner in driving that mission forward.

Responsibilities Include:

 - Technical Leadership: Provide strong technical leadership and vision for your engineering squad, ensuring alignment with the company's strategic goals — and staying close enough to the code to earn your team's respect

 - Technical Strategy: Collaborate with product management and other stakeholders to define and prioritize the technical roadmap, ensuring alignment with business objectives

 - Technical Standards: Implement processes, tools, and practices that drive high-velocity and high-quality delivery across onshore and offshore teams

 - Execution: Drive the successful delivery of software projects, including planning, resource allocation, and project management

 - Quality Assurance: Grow and foster our adoption of SQA best practices to ensure high-quality deliverables

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams (Product/Clinical/Data/ClientSuccess/etc.) and communicate effectively to distill technical details into business terms

notes: Healthcare tech startup with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
